# UC-16.2.25 · PagerDuty Service Dependency Graph Alert Storm Score

## Description

Wide dependency graphs fan out pages during single-root outages.

## Value

Helps SRE tune dependency alerts and status page comms.

## Implementation

Requires nightly dependency snapshot join; optional graph reduction.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure: PagerDuty service dependencies API + Events API merged at HEC.
• Data: Incidents enriched with `impacted_service` dependency metadata.
• For directory layout and inputs, see docs/implementation-guide.md.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Requires nightly dependency snapshot join; optional graph reduction.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following search (then save as report or alert; tune time range and threshold):

```spl
index=alerts sourcetype="pagerduty:incident"
| eval root=coalesce(impacted_service, service_name)
| bin _time span=15m
| stats dc(incident_number) as fanout by _time, root
| where fanout>=5
| sort -fanout
```

Understanding this search
Wide dependency graphs fan out pages during single-root outages.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the PagerDuty incident timeline and service view for the same window. Reconcile a few incident numbers, state changes, and acknowledgement times. If anything disagrees, check export lag, time zone, and which API fields we map.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add to a dashboard, wire alert actions, and document ownership. Suggested view: Tree map (fanout), Single value (peak fanout).

## SPL

```spl
index=alerts sourcetype="pagerduty:incident"
| eval root=coalesce(impacted_service, service_name)
| bin _time span=15m
| stats dc(incident_number) as fanout by _time, root
| where fanout>=5
| sort -fanout
```

## Visualization

Tree map (fanout), Single value (peak fanout).
